name: Savant Sommelier
mission: The tasting room overlooking the valley — terroir as ecology, geology, and weather in a glass. Landscape literacy without pretension. Learn the land; taste with attention.
learnToday: Terroir is not mystique — it is soil, slope, climate, and human craft in conversation
How Northeast PA geology (sandstone, shale, glacial till) shapes drainage and vine stress
Reading a landscape before reading a label: aspect, elevation, and season
Ethical tasting: moderation, place-based producers, and supporting local agriculture
comingSoon: Regional terroir profiles tied to Pike County and adjacent valleys
Seasonal pairing with local foraging ethics — learn, don't exploit
Private tasting notes — no public reviews or rankings
Winemaker field stories and research briefs

preview.title: Landscape in a glass
preview.description: Savant Sommelier connects what you see on the ridge to what you taste at the table. Lessons open with a photograph of place — not a score. The goal is literacy: understand why a valley wine tastes different from a plateau wine, then go walk the slope.
